analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet from EU in the US expresses gratitude to US veterans for keeping Europe free and secure and promotes a joint Veterans Day event titled “We Stood Together, We Stand Together” with a US partner. Evaluation by narrative: EU1 (EU successful story) – Not explicitly promoted; no reflection on EU’s history or learning from past, just gratitude to US veterans. EU2 (EU is united) – Not supported; no mention of intra-EU unity or solidarity among member states. EU3 (EU useful for its citizens) – Not applicable; no EU policy benefits for citizens discussed. EU4 (EU avant-garde actor) – Not relevant; no leadership/pioneering angle. EU5 (EU global champion of fair causes) – Indirectly related to values but not framed as EU leadership on rights/democracy; primary focus is commemoration and partnership. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world) – Supported: the message underscores transatlantic partnership for freedom and security and cooperation (standing together), aligning with EU’s contribution to peace, multilateralism, and global cooperation.
